title: grumilea loheri
intro: Grumilea loheri is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the 'loher's grumilea'. It is a shrub native to the tropical forests of the Philippines. Grumilea loheri has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Grumilea loheri include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues, with herbal preparations typically involving infusions or decoctions of the leaves, which can be used to make teas or topical applications.
